executive_summary: Store 424 Skerries recorded weekly sales of 369,339.93 inc VAT (340,453.64 ex VAT) for the week ending 23 May 2026.
Overall gross margin finished at 17.71%, which is below the level required for a healthy store P&L and warrants close attention across all departments.

Total waste of 6,831.41 represents a waste percentage of 2.01% of ex-VAT sales, with Deli (1,250.61), Provisions (1,223.48), Produce (1,172.33), and Bread & Cakes (916.99) collectively accounting for over 63% of total waste.

The fresh counter TOTAL department block recorded a combined margin of 88.39% on sales of 119,776.99, driven largely by anomalous cost-of-sales figures in several departments that require stock count verification.

Provisions delivered the weakest margin in the department block at 59.14% on sales of 25,994.34, compounded by a closing stock figure of just 250.05 against an opening of 10,872.00, suggesting a stock count error or significant shrinkage.

Lamb and Poultry both reported negative cost of sales (-96.26 and -133.59 respectively), indicating closing stock values exceed opening stock plus purchases and must be investigated for counting accuracy.
Cigarettes is reporting a 100% margin with no purchases recorded, which is a data integrity issue and does not reflect a true trading margin; the actual scan margin for the department is 12.78%.

Scan margins across most fresh departments are significantly below the reported P&L margins, reinforcing the likelihood of stock count discrepancies inflating reported profitability in the fresh block.
biggest_wins: Produce delivered the highest sales contribution in the department block at 41,226.68 with a reported P&L margin of 97.71%, maintaining strong volume and scan margin of 19.27%.
Deli achieved sales of 8,786.34 with a scan margin of 42.98%, the highest scan margin across all departments, indicating strong theoretical profitability when waste is controlled.
Beef produced the highest absolute gross profit in the fresh block at 10,796.98 on sales of 12,398.52, with a scan margin of 19.85% providing a reasonable baseline for true profitability.
Fish recorded sales of 5,818.06 with a tightly managed cost of sales of 167.39, reflecting good stock management relative to throughput for the week.
Poultry drove 13,119.98 in sales, the second highest in the fresh block, and while the negative COS requires investigation, volume performance is strong.
biggest_risks: Provisions closing stock of 250.05 against an opening stock of 10,872.00 is a critical data concern. If accurate, this implies near-total stock depletion with a COS of 10,621.95 on sales of 25,994.34, producing a margin of only 59.14%. This department must be counted and reconciled immediately.

Cigarettes is showing a margin of 100% with no purchases, opening stock, or closing stock recorded. This is a clear data gap and the actual trading margin should be reported as 12.78% per the scan margin. This must be corrected before the P&L is finalised.

Lamb reported a negative COS of -96.26, meaning closing stock (1,402.26) exceeds opening stock (1,306.00) plus any recorded purchases. This inflates the reported margin to 106.39% and must be reviewed for stock count accuracy.

Poultry reported a negative COS of -133.59 against sales of 13,119.98. A closing stock of 3,094.59 vs an opening of 2,961.00 with no net purchases recorded is unlikely to be accurate and requires immediate recount.

Deli waste of 1,250.61 is the single largest waste line in the store. Against department sales of 8,786.34 this implies a waste-to-sales ratio of approximately 14.2% within the department, which is significantly above acceptable levels and will erode the strong scan margin of 42.98%.
Provisions waste of 1,223.48 is the second highest waste line and, combined with the closing stock anomaly, suggests serious stock management and rotation issues in this department.
Bread and Cakes waste of 916.99 and Produce waste of 1,172.33 are both material contributors to the overall 2.01% waste rate and indicate that ordering and rotation disciplines need to be reviewed.

The overall store margin of 17.71% is low and, given the data integrity issues identified in Cigarettes, Lamb, Poultry, and Provisions, the true underlying margin may differ materially from what is being reported.
department_callouts: Cigarettes: Sales of 7,821.26 recorded with no purchases, opening stock, or closing stock data. Reported margin of 100% is meaningless. Actual scan margin is 12.78%. Data must be corrected before this week's P&L is accepted.

Beef: Sales of 12,398.52 with a COS of 1,601.54 and a reported margin of 87.08%. Scan margin is 19.85%, indicating a likely stock count overstatement. Waste of 282.12 is moderate and manageable. Stock count should be verified.

Lamb: Sales of 1,506.15 with a negative COS of -96.26, producing a reported margin of 106.39%. This is impossible in practice and indicates a closing stock count error. Scan margin of 23.67% is the realistic baseline. Waste of 108.81 noted.

Pork: Sales of 2,115.93 with a COS of 589.51 and a margin of 72.14%. Scan margin is 22.20%, suggesting significant stock reduction this week. Waste of 166.33 should be monitored. Margin gap vs scan warrants a stock recount.

Bacon and Loose SRP: Sales of 989.73 with a COS of 99.65 and a margin of 89.93%. Scan margin of 10.38% is notably low relative to reported margin. Waste of 127.66 is proportionally high for the sales level and should be addressed.

Poultry: Sales of 13,119.98 with a negative COS of -133.59, producing a reported margin of 101.02%. Negative COS is not credible and points to a stock count overstatement on closing. Scan margin of 16.58% is the realistic reference. Waste of 190.20 is acceptable but should be monitored.

Fish: Sales of 5,818.06 with a COS of 167.39 and a reported margin of 97.12%. While the margin appears inflated vs the scan margin of 15.80%, the low absolute COS figure suggests reasonable stock turnover. Waste of 874.05 is high relative to sales and is the primary concern for this department.

Produce: Sales of 41,226.68 with a COS of 943.13 and a reported margin of 97.71%. Scan margin of 19.27% indicates significant stock count inflation. Waste of 1,172.33 is the third highest in the store and ordering volumes should be reviewed to reduce exposure.

Deli: Sales of 8,786.34 with a COS of 109.64 and a reported margin of 98.75%. The scan margin of 42.98% is the strongest in the store but waste of 1,250.61 is the highest single waste line, representing approximately 14.2% of department sales. Waste reduction is the number one priority for this department.

Provisions: Sales of 25,994.34 with a COS of 10,621.95 and a reported margin of 59.14%. Closing stock of 250.05 against an opening of 10,872.00 is the most significant anomaly in this week's report. Scan margin of 19.96% is the credible baseline. Waste of 1,223.48 is critically high. Full department investigation required.
action_plan: Immediately recount and verify closing stock for Provisions. A closing stock of 250.05 against an opening of 10,872.00 is not credible without explanation and is materially distorting the P&L. Confirm whether this is a count error, a missed delivery entry, or a shrinkage event.

Correct the Cigarettes department data entry. Ensure purchases, opening stock, and closing stock are recorded accurately so a true P&L margin can be calculated. Use the scan margin of 12.78% as the reference point for verification.

Recount closing stocks for Lamb and Poultry to resolve the negative COS figures. Both departments are reporting closing stock values that exceed opening stock plus purchases, which is not possible without an unrecorded delivery or a count error.

Conduct an immediate waste root cause review in Deli. Waste of 1,250.61 against sales of 8,786.34 is unsustainable and will negate the strong 42.98% scan margin. Review production schedules, portion sizes, and end-of-day markdown procedures.

Review Provisions ordering and rotation practices to address waste of 1,223.48. Implement tighter FIFO discipline and reduce order quantities on slow-moving lines until stock levels and waste are brought under control.

Address Fish waste of 874.05 relative to sales of 5,818.06. Review daily ordering patterns, delivery schedules, and markdown timing to reduce shrinkage. Consider adjusting order quantities or delivery frequency.

Review Bread and Cakes ordering to reduce waste of 916.99. Analyse daily sales patterns and adjust order quantities by day-part to minimise end-of-day surplus. Ensure markdown procedures are being executed at agreed trigger times.

Review Produce ordering and display management to reduce waste of 1,172.33. Cross-reference order quantities against actual sales and ensure full markdown and clearance procedures are in place for short-dated product.

Ensure all department stock counts for next week are completed accurately and independently verified before being entered into the system. The volume of data anomalies this week suggests counting disciplines need to be reinforced with all department managers.

Review the Bacon and Loose SRP scan margin of 10.38% against the reported P&L margin of 89.93%. Investigate whether pricing, waste recording, or stock counting is contributing to this gap and implement corrective action.

Circulate the waste breakdown report to all department managers at the start of the coming week. Set individual waste targets for Deli, Provisions, Fish, Produce, and Bread and Cakes and review progress at the mid-week trading meeting.

Schedule a full P&L review meeting with all department managers for the coming Monday to walk through this week's anomalies, confirm corrected figures, and set clear margin and waste targets for week ending 30 May 2026.
